items.title: Concerns for lost health services under sale to Calvary

children.content: Doctors are concerned Tasmanians will be forced to travel interstate for certain procedures if the Hobart Private Hospital is sold to Catholic provider Calvary, but the health minister insists there will be no service gap.

gallery.altText: Exterior signage at Hobart Private Hospital.

thumbnail.altText: Exterior signage at Hobart Private Hospital.

items.title: Paramedics told not to take complex cardiac patients to Mersey hospital

children.content: Paramedics have been told to transport complex cardiac patients in Tasmania's north-west to the Launceston General Hospital rather than the local Mersey Community Hospital.

items.title: Despite barely any population growth, Tasmania doesn't have enough houses

children.content: Despite having the slowest growing population in the country, Tasmania is still battling rising homelessness and an ever-tightening rental market.

image.altText: A house model and a key.

items.title: Asylum seekers sent to Nauru by Australia say they are going hungry

children.content: According to a survey of 78 asylum seekers on Nauru, 99 per cent said they were regularly skipping meals because they could not afford to buy enough food, while more than three quarters said they rarely had access to free, clean drinking water.

gallery.altText: A coastline with ship-loading equipment. The ocean is in the foreground and there are trees on the land.
Refugees and asylum seekers wander around the detention centre on Nauru

thumbnail.altText: A coastline with ship-loading equipment. The ocean is in the foreground and there are trees on the land.
Refugees and asylum seekers wander around the detention centre on Nauru

items.title: US ally Jordan summons Iranian ambassador after missile strikes

children.content: The US warns its citizens to stay away from air and sea ports at Aqaba in southern Jordan, hours before Israeli authorities announced that Iranian missiles had been launched towards the city.

image.altText: A satellite imagery of an air base with helicopters and planes.

items.title: Rules to tighten on AI use in decision-making by government departments

children.content: Agencies like Centrelink and Services Australia will have to follow a framework to ensure decision-making is "fair and accurate" if they use AI.

items.title: Actor Terence Donovan remembered by son Jason as 'larger than life'

children.content: Jason Donovan has paid tribute to his father who has died at the age of 90 after decades on Australian screens.

gallery.altText: Three men pose for the camera smiling and laughing

items.title: Antonelli extends F1 championship lead, heartbroken Russell crashes out in Belgium

children.content: Kimi Antonelli has extended his lead at the top of the F1 championship standings with victory in the Belgian Grand Prix, with Oscar Piastri overcoming an early knock and George Russell's race ending in heartbreak.

image.altText: A man with dark curly hair raises his arm and smiles while wearing a racing jacket

items.title: The 'terrifying' lab test results from store-bought bottle of vodka

children.content: Australia's bootleg booze market is booming and an alarming new discovery from a bottle of vodka sold in Melbourne reveals it is turning into a major health problem.

gallery.altText: A large bottle of vodka sitting in a tray for lab testing.

thumbnail.altText: A large bottle of vodka sitting in a tray for lab testing.

items.title: Russia pounds Kyiv in major ballistic missile attack

children.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Russia battered Ukraine's capital Kyiv overnight into Sunday local time, with one of its biggest ballistic missile barrages of the war.

image.altText: A plume of dark grey smoke hangs in the sky above a busy city road, as cars drive past.

items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested on sex trafficking offences

children.content: British prosecutors say they are seeking the extradition of the Tate brothers from the US on rape and sex trafficking charges.

image.altText: Tristan and Andrew Tate pictured sitting down in the audience at an event with serious expressions

items.title: Special guest dazzles aspiring astronauts at Space Day Out

children.content: Young Australians got one step closer to achieving their space exploration dreams on Sunday when they met a "real-life" astronaut.

items.title: Bek never thought she would grow old until she left her church behind

children.content: Author Bek Condello says she has not spoken to her friends or family inside the church since she decided to leave.

image.altText: A young woman has her blonde hair tied up, wears a white singlet, sunnies, looks to the side, venice domes behind.

items.title: There's anti-aging hype. Then there's the science showing real promise

children.content: The fear of aging is driving a wave of biohacking businesses promising to help restore youth and fight disease. Four Corners investigates the race to reverse the aging process.

image.altText: a blonde woman lying in a white pod

items.title: How can you get the balance right when being online is your job?

children.content: When being across trends in your industry feels like part of your job, it can feel like work never ends. Here's how to protect your free time.

children.content: From Mexican wrestling to Indian slums, catch up on every week seven film from Race Around the World.

items.title: Love story: Inseparable since meeting, these besties are planning retirement together

children.content: Tania Differding and Ella Crofts met as teenagers and have been inseparable since. The best friends, now aged 43, are planning the rest of their lives around one another.

items.title: Boys who embrace 'expansive masculinity' likely to fare better

children.content: These men are being the role models they never had. They hope to curb Australia's masculinity crisis.

image.altText: a young boy smiles at the camera, while hugging his dad whose face we can't see

items.title: Curious case of absent US senator reignites elderly politicians debate

children.content: Washington's rumour mill is hyperactive at the best of times. But an elderly senator's lengthy absence from Congress is cranking it into frenzied overdrive.

image.altText: Mitch McConnell sits in a hospital bed with his wife by his side.

items.title: Hinkley set to coach Devils, Caddy taken to hospital as Dons end drought

children.content: Tasmania prepares to unveil Ken Hinkley as its inaugural AFL coach as Essendon snaps a 12-game losing streak despite losing Nate Caddy early.

image.altText: Ken Hinkley speaking to reporters at a Power media conference.

items.title: Tom Trbojevic gutted after another injury, Titans, Dolphins record wins

children.content: The Dolphins shook off last week's humiliating thrashing to power past the Cowboys 36-16 at Lang Park, after Tom Trbojevic suffered another hamstring injury in Manly's defeat to Gold Coast.

items.title: 'Why do you care how I choose to dress?': Boxer on critics of her hijab

children.content: Ahead of her professional debut, Tina Rahimi sat down for a lengthy interview about representation, the Islamophobia she encounters, and re-learning her identity after an Olympics heartbreak.

thumbnail.altText: Tina Rahimi poses ahead of her pro boxing debut

items.title: Brondello suspended by WNBA for inappropriate comment

children.content: Australian coach Sandy Brondello apologises to WNBA star Angel Reese after she is suspended for one game by the league.

image.altText: Sandy Brondello coaching the Toronto Tempo during a WNBA game.

items.title: Expert says new cyber law shows Vic government is out of touch with reality

children.content: Jacinta Allan is pledging nation-leading laws forcing tech giants to reveal the identity of trolls, and make it easier for parents to sue tech giants that cause psychiatric harm to their children.

image.altText: Close up of a hand typing on a keyboard in a dark room

items.title: Hundreds gather in Alice Springs ahead of Pine Gap protest

children.content: Hundreds of activists and academics have gathered in Alice Springs to join Arrernte traditional owners ahead of a planned protest against the joint Australian-US military base at Pine Gap.

items.title: Outback tour company sues vehicle manufacturer over fatal NT rollover

children.content: An Alice Springs tour company is suing a vehicle manufacturer for negligence and breach of contract following a bus crash that killed a 69-year-old man in 2022.

image.altText: Patrick Bedford from Tourism Central Australia says JobKeeper needs to be extended

items.title: 'My heart is empty': Millions of Chinese forced to end AI relationships

children.content: A boyfriend betrayed 17-year-old Xiaoxue, so she made an AI partner. But now the government is trying to end her relationship.
